summary: Good matrix mix captures a killer 20‑minute Eyes of the World jam despite some fence‑climbing crowd noise.
review: Both reviewers highlight the standout, roughly 20‑minute standalone "Eyes of the World" that makes this show a must‑listen. The recording is a matrix blend that carries noticeable audience chatter (fence‑climbers) but remains clear enough to enjoy the fast, “80s‑style” energy of the band. Overall the performance is praised as one of the great shows from the period.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance with a highlighted long Eyes jam and generally positive comments, offset by limited detail on recording quality and only two reviews.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix mix includes noticeable crowd noise (people climbing the fence) that some listeners find distracting, but overall clarity is sufficient and the sound has a slightly “jacked‑up” 80s character.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is driven by an extended, high‑energy Eyes of the World that keeps momentum despite occasional crowd distractions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Crazy finger work noted in the performance.
